brief_justification: Paper A is a data-driven Bayesian inference pipeline (hydrodynamical simulations feeding an emulator, then MCMC) that constrains a dark matter-neutrino scattering strength from Lyman-alpha forest observations, whereas paper B is an analytical construction of regular axisymmetric Kerr-like black hole metrics in general relativity; simulation-based statistical parameter estimation and exact spacetime-geometry construction are entirely different machinery with no role-for-role correspondence.
